title: Dealer Upset After Losing a Bid Due to Pricing
openingLine: We just lost the State University job. Your stuff was too expensive — competitor came in $80K under us on the same scope. We need better pricing if we're going to keep proposing your line. This isn't the first time it's happened.
customerContext: You're frustrated and a little embarrassed. You championed Wenger to the university and got beat by a competitor on price. You're not sure if the loss was actually about Wenger pricing or about your own sales approach, but you're more comfortable blaming the manufacturer. You've been a Wenger dealer for 12 years and you feel like you're owed something.

hiddenInfo.reveal: Wenger replacement was Norton Acoustics. We're not sure if it was apples-to-apples on warranty or testing.
Honestly looking back, our install timeline was also longer than theirs. Owner cared about that too.
I'm not sure the owner fully understood the differences. We didn't do a great job on the value pitch.
We didn't really know the budget going in — we were quoting blind based on the spec.
University procurement and the music department chair. Procurement made the final call on price.
The university has owned Wenger for years. They were happy with the legacy stuff.
We haven't done formal Wenger product training in about three years. Newer reps don't know the line well.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked which competitor was used and whether it was apples-to-apples
Asked whether price was the only factor or if other elements played in
Asked whether the university has bought Wenger before
Asked whether the owner understood the differences

Solution Framing.behavior: Reframed the value: warranty, durability, life-cycle costs

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Proposed a pre-bid strategy for future opportunities
Identified what solution the owner really wanted

BANT.behavior: Confirmed who ultimately made the decision
Asked whether the budget was known going in

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Set follow-up tasks for any post-mortem actions

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Educated the dealer on Wenger's process for the future
Reviewed the project together — what we'd do differently
Committed to get owner buy-in on the next opportunity

Other.behavior: Documented all conversations and competitive pricing in Salesforce
Offered product training or tools to help dealer sell on value
